marketState: neutral, tilted cautiously bullish — fairly priced with a bullish weather/positioning skew offset by a storage surplus
summary: NG is trading almost exactly at model fair value ($2.96 vs $2.92 fair now, spread only +$0.039) across every horizon out to +21D, so there is no mispricing edge to trade on price-vs-fair alone. The 8-16 day weather outlook is the standout signal: demand is +46.8% vs the 5-year norm and above the historical max range, with the demand trend accelerating (+23% vs 5y normal, +14.4 in 24h) — a genuine bullish cooling-demand impulse. That's reinforced by managed-money futures positioning sitting at just the 8th percentile of the last 5 years (net -105.7k contracts), an unusually crowded short that raises short-covering risk. Working against this: storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise, keeping the market structurally well-supplied, and daily/weekly technicals remain bearish (RSI 39-44, EMA 50<200) even as 5m/15m intraday momentum is bullish. Seasonality is a mild net positive (price -3.4pp below its typical-year median, 69% odds of being higher in 4 weeks) but the 63pp reliability spread means it should only lightly influence sizing this week.

longTerm.entry: $2.90 - $2.97 (scale in near spot / on dips toward fair value)
longTerm.rationale: The 8-16D weather signal (well above the 5y max range, still accelerating) plus a heavily net-short/oversold COT position (8th percentile) argue for upside into the next several weeks, and price sits below its seasonal median with fair value itself drifting up ($2.92 now to $3.00 by +21D). Storage surplus and low seasonality reliability cap conviction at medium rather than high.
longTerm.invalidation: 8-16D heat forecast fades back toward normal, storage surprises keep coming in bearish, or price closes convincingly below the ~$2.75-2.80 area (below the recent weather/technical support zone).

shortTerm.rationale: Price is FAIR at both now and +7D (spreads inside +$0.04), so there is no near-term valuation edge, and technicals are internally conflicted — 5m/15m bullish (EMA 50>200, MACD positive) but 1h shows MACD negative and 1d/1w are outright bearish. Near-term weather (1-7D) is only modestly below normal (-4.5%), not a strong directional driver.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a confirmed break and hold above ~$3.05-3.10 (1h/4h EMA flipping bullish, aligning with the intraday trend) for a momentum long, or a pullback into the ~$2.80-2.85 zone (fair-value support, still above the long-term stop) for a better risk/reward entry.

Managed-money COT net short at just the 8th 5y-percentile — crowded short, short-covering risk
Price trading FAIR vs model fair value across now/+7D/+14D/+21D, but -3.4pp below seasonal median

Technicals diverge by timeframe: 5m/15m bullish vs 1d/1w bearish (RSI 39-44, EMA 50<200)
risks: Seasonality reliability is Low (63pp 10-90% spread) — the historical seasonal pattern has wide dispersion this week
